results.title: The Hidden curriculum: assumptions
results.creator_credit: Janet Isaac, Bruce Moir and Film Australia
results.brief_description: This short film revfeals some of the effects of sex role stereotyping and is intended to trigger group discussion. Children of different age groups ranging from kindergarten to senior secondary students refveal their attitudes to girls, mothers and the female role. Parents have higher career expectations of their sons than of their daughters. Suitable for upper secondary and tertiary levels.

results.title: The Hickory stick
results.creator_credit: Irving Jacoby, Mental Health Film Board and National Education Association
results.brief_description: Demonstrates how a fifth grade teacher maintains an orderly atmosphere for learning and at the same time helps her youngsters cope with their behaviour and learning problems. Shows how she uses firmness and understanding in helping children to control their feelings and accept authority.

results.title: Heysen on Heysen
results.creator_credit: John Dick, Lesley Hammond, Milton Ingerson Film and Television Productions and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: A filmic retrospective of a selection of paintings of Sir Hans Heysen, many from private collections, that reveal Heysen the man. Very lyrical, both visually and vocally.

results.title: He's not the walking kind
results.creator_credit: Sandra Wilson, Peter Jones and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The story of a young man who, despite a crippling malady is determined to experience as many of life’s offerings as possible. Brian Wilson is a spastic, confined to a wheelchair, but he works at a job, looks after himself, moving about from place to place on his own. Every day has its challenges and victories and sometimes defeats. An insight into the private daily struggle of the handicapped.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Sandra Wilson, Peter Jones and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Herzberg
results.creator_credit: John McAulay and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Gerhard Herzberg, winner of a Nobel Prize in 1971, is a molecular spectroscopist. This film shows Dr. Herzberg in his laboratory at the National Research Council in Ottawa where, with the aid of highly sophisticated instruments, he tracks down elusive bits of matter that are the keys to discovering what the planets, stars and the universe are made of.

results.title: The Heroes
results.brief_description: A boy with a creative impulse is exposed to the influence of war toys through his companion and through commercialism. His conscience forces him to resist. He cannot decide what is better, reation or destruction. He eventually finds destruction more amusing than appreciation or creation, he abandons his drawing pad and runs off to play war. Music composed and sung by Glen Tomasetti.

results.title: Herman holds a sales meeting
results.brief_description: A sales manager holds a sales meeting and explains his activities and operations. Flashbacks show what he really did and said. A light-hearted treatment which will counter-balance the serious side of any sales meeting.

results.title: Heritage of hops
results.creator_credit: Phillip Mark-Law, Don Anderson and Tasmanian State Film Unit
results.brief_description: The charm of the early days of hop growing in Tasmania has been preserved in many hop buildings. Modern hop growing and processing techniques are now highly sophisticated. Hops are used in beer brewing to bitter the beer.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Phillip Mark-Law, Don Anderson and Tasmanian State Film Unit
